This is a 40-pack set of mason jars with: - 30 sliver lids with rims - 10 golden lids with rims - 1 tiny brush - 40 chalkboard labels with chalk marker  I only ever used Ball jars, lids, and rims in the past and now I know why. I had an opportunity to try this and CANNOT recommend them.  I spent hours water bath canning only to discover that the golden lids DO NOT SEAL at all--that is 25% of the jars.  In the fourth photo, the gold rim and lid is to the left of the picture. The silver lids appear to seal but the rims on about 90% of the jars are almost impossible to screw on. They are very sticky. After having the bad experience with the golden lids and using 14 of the jars with lids and rims, I decided to use my Ball canning set instead . When using a Ball rim, they glide on these jars. I have a funny feeling that because the rims are so bad, that the seal is broken periodically as a result of unscrewing the rim and checking if the lid is holding. For the golden lids, they just do not seal at all, whether the rims are good or not.  I see no reason for the tiny brush. The 40 chalkboard labels are very nice as they cover the entire lid and peel off and stick on easily (seventh photo). The chalk marker is okay. The jars themselves are good. If you find yourself stuck with this garbage, just go out and buy Ball lids and rims that will fit these jars and they will become usable. I do not trust these lids and rims at all--TERRIBLE.  If sealing a jar during canning is not a concern, then these jars, lids, and rims are usable even if the rim is hard to screw on. However, I only use canning jars for hot water bath canning, so I have to throw out all these lids and rims.  I would personally never buy this set after having tried it. The free brush, labels, and marker can always be purchased separately. Stick with Ball which is priced not much more than this and works every time.

I have a very good canister vacuum for "cleaning day" and just wanted something easier/lighter to take around the apartment for quicker/easier vacuuming without dragging the canister and wire around.  I checked out reviews of quite a few stick vacuums before this purchase.  I couldn't believe how expensive some of them are.  I mentioned to my sister that I was looking for a stick vacuum and she told me she just got one on Amazon, had only used it a couple of times and couldn't say much about it other than it was quiet, good suction, light weight and only $99.  She got it because several ladies in her apt. building had one and were happy with it.  I decided to take a chance and ordered one for myself.  It came in a day, in good shape. It was easy enough to put together.  It's lightweight, pretty quiet and easy to empty.  The head swivels and gets into corners. I don't think it would last long with an aggressive house cleaner.  The swivel head might not be strong enough to be slammed into walls or corners.  I haven't yet used the other tools it came with, yet.  The suction is very good and I used it mostly on the low setting, but I don't have pets or children and my floors are laminate or have low pile carpeting so I can't say how it would be on thick carpets, etc. The high power just shortens the battery life. I was happy that the suction was strong enough to pick up crumbs, etc. and didn't just fling bits out the other side.  The battery lasted about 30-40 minutes, enough to do my whole apartment with good suction.  It takes about 4 hrs to charge the battery, so I wanted to purchase an extra battery to have charged and on hand.  It's $33...a third of the vacuum cost...Wow!  I haven't used it enough to comment on the quality (how long it lasts, parts break, or suction weakens) but so far, I'm very happy with the vacuum.  It serves its purpose well.  If need be, down the line, I'll add to or change this review.

This budget-priced (about $40 on Amazon) DIRT DEVIL POWER EXPRESS LITE PET 3-IN-1 CORDED STICK VACUUM (SD22020B) has only moderate suction, and doesn't “pick up” well on carpeted floors, even though it has a powered brush.  When I tried it on carpeted stairs, the motor filters became clogged with fine fuzz almost immediately, and the vac began dropping fuzz back on the carpet.  (The troubleshooting guide says this will happen if the motor filter is clogged.)  The filter is easy to remove and clean (by brushing it off), but it's very small, without much capacity for fine dust or lint.  I can't imagine using this vac for a large carpeted area.  My household has no pets, the stairs are vacuumed fairly often, and the vac picked up only a small amount of hair--yet the filter needed cleaning after only a few minutes of operation.  The larger dirt cannister is extremely easy to remove and empty, but it collected almost nothing from my carpeted stairs.  I wanted this vac to clean areas that my Roomba 650 cannot reach, and I suppose it will work for that.  However, I was disappointed in this model, because Dirt Devil is a brand that I have come to trust.  A Dirt Devil detailer, a Dirt Devil hand vac, and a Dirt Devil upright vac have all performed very well for me in the past.

This KITCHEN GIZMO SNAP N STRAIN STRAINER (Fits all Pots and Bowls; Lime Green) does NOT fit my Cuisinart saucepans or my Instant Pot stainless steel liner.  The claim that it “fits all pots and bowls” is incorrect.  The problem is that the strainer is pre-formed to a certain curve, so that it leaves a big gap at the bottom between the strainer and the saucepan/pot edge if the strainer and pan curves don’t exactly match.  This means that the pan contents slide right out through the gap when you try to strain off grease or water.  I purchased this item in hopes of using it to strain grease from my Instant Pot.  However, it doesn’t come close to matching the curve of the Instant Pot liner, so it is useless for this purpose.  It is also useless for straining water off pasta cooked in a Cuisinart saucepan, because the curves don’t match.  The clips are clumsy to use, especially if you are trying to clip the Gizmo onto a hot pan.  In short, the Gizmo doesn’t work nearly as well as [[ASIN:B0021Y9O1O an inexpensive plastic colander]] for straining veggies, pasta, or anything else cooked in boiling water.

Water Reservoir, and Thermal Flavor Extraction (CE201), Black/Stainless Steel) made excellent coffee for me for about 9 months.  My household drinks a lot of coffee, so the coffee maker was used for an average of two pots per day.  Today, I made a second pot and left the carafe on the warming plate as usual.  An hour or so later, the coffee maker began softly beeping.  It seemed to have spontaneously attempted to brew another pot of coffee--but of course the water reservoir was empty.  The pot smelled hot, like something was wrong with the electrical parts.  I tried turning the coffee maker off and on, but the beeping persisted.  Ditto after I unplugged the coffee maker and turned it on again.  Obviously the coffee maker is now unsafe to use, even if the beeping stops after the coffee maker cools down.  There is nothing in the instruction booklet about this steady, soft beeping warning.  I’m glad that the beeping alerted me to a problem, but I wonder what would have happened if I hadn’t been home when it started up.  Unfortunately, I liked this coffee maker so much, I purchased a backup.  I still plan to use the backup, but I will be afraid to leave it plugged in or turned on when I’m not close by.  This is an issue, because unplugging the coffee maker cancels the programming, and turning it off cancels the stay warm feature.  I will NOT be purchasing another of these expensive coffee makers.  Because the pot was so heavily used, I can accept a 9-month lifespan.  But all of my other coffee makers have simply stopped working when they were worn out.  This one was burning out, with some kind of electrical malfunction.

These ALL-WEATHER PATIO TABLE placemats are made from the same sort of material found in certain rolls of soft, nonslip, "gripper" shelf liner.  They're suitable for outdoor patio table use, but not nearly attractive enough for everyday indoor table use.  At the price (about $15), I wasn't expecting much, but I was still quite disappointed.  The material is so full of holes, spilled liquids go right through.  No easy cleanup with these mats!  Also, the rubbery texture of the mat "fabric" is mildly unpleasant to touch.  The mats do provide cushioning for ceramic dinnerware placed on hard table surfaces.  What I was looking for, was an inexpensive, protective, wipe-clean placemat for everyday use on a round dining-room table.  After I ordered these placemats, I found some suitable rectangular ones in solid vinyl at Bed, Bath and Beyond.  I converted the BB&Y mats into wedge-shaped placemats by using ordinary scissors to cut them into shape.  Because of the cheap shelf-liner material, I rate these All-Weather Patio Table placemats at 2 stars ("I don't like it" on the official Amazon scale).

After my much-loved Hamilton Beach Digital 4-Slice Toaster developed a short (after long use), I purchased this HAMILTON BEACH 4-SLICE EXTRA WIDE SLOT TOASTER (with Defrost and Bagel Functions, Shade Selector, Toast Boost, Auto-Shutoff and Cancel Button, Black (24633)) as a replacement.  What a disappointment!  It’s hard to select the toasting time, because the printed shade selector numbers and pointer are small and faint.  Frozen bread barely toasts when the shade selector is set to 6 or the maximum 7.  The toaster toasts the bread unevenly (only the top half), and mostly toasts only on one side.  When I toasted English muffins, one muffin failed to pop up at the end of the cycle, even though the muffin went in smoothly.  The crumb trays, which should be emptied weekly, are inconveniently placed.  To empty them, you must unplug the toaster and turn it upside down over a sink or garbage receptacle.  Because I’ve owned many excellent Hamilton Beach toasters in the past, I have to wonder whether this particular toaster model is a counterfeit knockoff.  The design flaws I can live with, but the performance is barely there when the toaster is brand new.

So far, so good.  We haven't had bed bugs, but know people who have.  Plus we travel with fair frequency and they are everywhere.  The bug oven is an insurance policy.  It can be REALLY expensive to eradicate if you end up bringing them into your home.  It does take up a fair bit of real estate in our garage, but I think it's well spent space.  My husband is handy - he can build things and do electrical stuff... he built a simple stand for the oven to sit on - not too high so you can lift suitcases and such into it but keeps it off the epoxied floor of our garage.  An "x" shape plywood stand with a top that is big enough for the ovens to sit on opposite sides. Your oven comes with 4 yoga blocks (foam bricks) to make sure the heated air is circulating and you get a sensor to put inside your bags - and then a sensor reader that sits outside the oven - allowing you to see what the temp inside is without opening the oven.  We have been able to heat things thoroughly through within  about 2 or 3 hours.  The heat kills all stages.  Haven't seen a single one... but I feel much better about taking things into the house AFTER the oven.  You do need to plug in heaters into plugs on separate circuits - or you will pop breakers every time... they also include a heavy duty 25' ft extension cord to make the transition.  We leave ours up in order to be more apt to use it - but like I said, we travel quite a lot.  So far so good , however,  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I am utterly disappointed in these pillowcases. First of all, I have a king-sized bed with king-sized pillows and some standard pillows. I ordered these in a king. However, the fit is a small standard and it is not even a good fit because the flap of the sham has too tiny a pocket to fit over the end of the pillow. Another negative is that these show and hold onto a lot of lint and cat hair.  Per my Internet search, these are the typical pillow sizes and the cases should be equivalent.  Standard Pillow Size 20" x 26" Super Standard Pillow Size 20" x 28" Queen Pillow Size 20" x 30" King Pillow Size 20" x 36"  The box says these are King sized at 20" x 26" which is a full 10" shorter than needed. As can be seen in the photo, my king-sized pillow comes beyond the case. In the other photo, my standard pillow is even peeking out due to the cuff pocket being way too short.  The only thing good about these cases is the color and the feel. However, these are not functional due to the odd sizing and lack of correct pocket size for the end of the sham. The current price of $29.99 is outrageous for a substandard product. If it were perfect in sizing and sham pocket, then I would have rated this 5 stars and considered the price competitive.

This is what could have been a good four-compartment organizer for different types of Ziplock bags (quart, gallon, sandwich, and snack) except that the vendor label is etched into the bamboo just like the bag labels are etched into the bamboo. This is a BIG NEGATIVE for me as it makes the unit look like garbage. Second, there is a crack along the backboard of the unit (see photo).  This organizer opens via a hinge from the clip in the front so that the bags can be loaded easily in addition to being able to load them through the holes. This can be hung on the wall or be placed in a drawer that is large enough to hold it. Rubberized circles are provided which I installed. This keeps this from sliding around or scratching a drawer or wall. Three screws with plastic mollies are also provided for hanging.  Overall, this is a good organizer that does away with all those squished and ugly boxes in my cabinets. I would have rated this 4 stars but the vendor name etched into the bamboo, the crazy price, as well as the crack in the back, has me taking off two additional stars. The vendor label irks me the most as the crack is small. They should have a removable stick-on label on the back instead if they need to advertise their product. Also, the current price of $39.99 is also ridiculously expensive. In fact, this price is about $10 more than comparable units sell for on the market.  There are many other Ziplock bag organizer options available such as this [[ASIN:B09KMNHM2J one]] or this [[ASIN:B09VGP1WRL one]]. For one full unit only, then I recommend this [[ASIN:B09RF759BM rustic organizer]]. For a more modern look, I recommend this [[ASIN:B09WN5PZ6Q one]] or an individual heavy plastic set like [[ASIN:B09THNTBGX this]]. For a cheaper unit that has room for foil and plastic wrap, then I highly recommend this [[ASIN:B0B1TPD21S one]] but no screws are provided with that one. I tried all of these and the style purchased is based on what one desires. I personally like these other ones a lot better than this one.

Note that I also purchased the [[ASIN:B01AAHHB5E Ball Jelly Elite Collection Jam Jar (4 Pack), 8 oz, Clear]] as well and love those jars and would purchase that version of the Elite collection but not these colored ones. What a disappointment.  Compare my photo with the ad photo.  The ad photo shows a light beige-ish contrast color with the blues.  That is NOT what you get - the yellow is very bright and only on the dinner plate.  The colors are NOT muted at all.  Further, the bread plates have a different color palate so they almost look they don't go with the dinner plates. You get bread (or what they call "appetizer" plates) cereal bowls and dinner plates - 6 of each.  NO cups.  The bowls get hopelessly stuck if you stack them!  I've never had a Corelle set do that. Design flaw?  I have to place paper towels between each one.  What a nightmare.  Further, one of the dinner plates was chipped, leaving a very sharp edge - so it had to be tossed for safety.  No loose chip was found in the packaging so it appears it may have been factory packed that way.  Honestly, I was really hoping for a more modern looking print - but with the garish yellow it is really cheap looking and retro - but NOT in a good way.  So disappointing.  I love Corelle dinnerware - as it is tough and yet lightweight.  Had high hopes but this stuff is really ugly in person. There are no complimentary pieces (besides "appetizer" plates) - so no mugs or cups or even salad size plates.  I wish I could box them back up... and return them - but the packaging could not be recovered to do that. I will likely donate them and learn an expensive lesson. Only purchase the design you have seen in person.

My 19 year old son need to replace his desk chair. He is more than thrilled with this one. It was very easy to assemble. It took my husband less than fifteen minutes to put it together. My son especially likes that the tilt feature is controlled. He doesn't like much tilting, so he has it adjusted so it's not tilting at all. It's easier for him to work at his desk that way. His old chair tilted too far back and it couldn't be adjusted. The chair is sturdy, too.  **Update: After 2 months, both of the arms of the chair have broken. I guess it's off to the dumpster for this chair.

This BEYOND GROUP 1 33 DIGITAL INSTANT READ THERMOCOUPLE THERMOMETER (with Folding Probe) has one great feature--a “Hold” button that locks the reading so you can read it after you remove the probe.  However, it is NOT an “instant read” thermometer--when your fingers heat the probe a little by unfolding it, the display is very slow to return to a room temperature reading.  Also, It takes many seconds to reach a stable reading after the probe is inserted into hot food.  The thermometer gives readings that are a couple of degrees higher than my expensive [[ASIN:B01M6V1SXE Thermapen]] (which does give instant readings).  You’ll want to test this thermometer in boiling water, to determine what the degree readings really mean, if you’re planning to use it to test cooked meats for safety.  The default display shows Centigrade numbers; if you want Fahrenheit, you must press the “Hold” button for 3 seconds.  You must reset to Fahrenheit frequently--whenever you power on the thermometer, and whenever it shuts off automatically (after a fairly short time).  The “Hold” button works as promised.  When the temperature is locked, the display shows the last reading.  (The display blinks when it’s reading temperatures.)  However, the “Hold” feature isn’t enough to overcome the thermometer’s other deficiencies.

This PHILIPS SMOKE-LESS GRILL (plus Bonus Cleaning Tool, HD6371/98) is very expensive (about $280 on Amazon) for what it is.  It cooks with two banks of infrared lights, but that’s NOT why it’s “smokeless”.  It’s smokeless, more or less, because the drip tray is located well below the grill surface, and below the heat sources, so that grease drippings don’t burn hard onto the drip pan (causing smoke).  If you’ve ever broiled a steak in an electric oven, you know that most of the smoke comes from grease that is burning onto the broiler pan surface nearest the broiler coils. Thus, with this grill, you’ll still get some smoke if you cook fatty foods.  My appliance didn’t melt at the sides with first use, but there are a lot of things to NOT like about this appliance.  First, the handles on the barbecue grid (the loose tray on top) get very hot at the four points where the metal joins the plastic handle insulation.  Surprise for the unwary cook!  Second, although there’s a warning against staring at the infrared lights, it’s impossible NOT to look at them, because they’re located right below the barbecue grid.  You have to look down on them when you turn the food or check for doneness.  Third, the grill takes 6-7 minutes to preheat, and it sends a lot of heat into your kitchen.  You definitely don’t want to use the appliance underneath your kitchen cabinets (danger of setting them on fire).  My electric stove’s broiler doesn’t heat the kitchen as much.  The appliance also takes quite a while to cool down so that it can be safely cleaned.  Fourth, the appliance is big and awkward to handle.  It takes up a lot of countertop or table space.  Fifth, the drip tray slides out from the left end, so it needs a lot of space for removal.  Sixth, the appliance isn’t easy to clean, except for the barbecue grid and the drip tray (both are dishwasher safe).  Grease spatters on the insides of the appliance, and must be scraped off.  Otherwise, the grease will burn on and start to produce smoke.  Finally, the scraper tool is just a paint scraper with double-edged razor blades (you get three blades).  These are readily available at hardware stores.  I’m still scratching my head at the price.  I wanted this because my electric oven’s broiler produces some smoke, and because it burns grease onto the broiler pan so badly that I have to clean the pan with Easy-Off.  But the broiler and broiler pan are much easier (and safer) to use and to clean than this grilling appliance.

This MR.COFFEE 12-CUP PROGRAMMABLE COFFEEMAKER, STAINLESS STEEL looks fabulous, brews fast, has easy-to-use buttons, includes a water filtration system, has a keep-warm plate, and includes a cleaning cycle setting.  Unfortunately, the coffeemaker also has a hard-to-fill reservoir, a clumsy "removable filter basket", and an exposed showerhead-type water system that emits steam when the lid is opened.  The good looks are not enough to make me like this coffeemaker!  Everything (coffee grounds and water) must be added from the top.  This is all right when the coffeemaker is cold.  However, if you open the top to add coffee for a second pot right after the first pot has brewed, the showerhead will send a cloud of steam straight into your face.  (I strongly prefer a coffeemaker with an enclosed showerhead and a coffee basket that swings out sideways, like the slower-brewing [[ASIN:B0041A5H5S Hamilton Beach 12-Cup Digital Coffee Maker]].)  The lid on this Mr. Coffee coffeemaker has a second lid in the back half that flips up.  I think this is intended for you to use in filling the reservoir, but the second lid doesn't always want to stay up.  It's also faster to fill the reservoir by opening the entire lid.  When you open the top, the entire mechanism with the showerhead feels flimsy, as if it will break easily (and early breakage has been my experience with this type of coffeemaker design).  The outer "removable filter basket" lacks a handle for easy removal.  It has ear-like tabs that are hard to grip (especially if the coffeemaker is hot) and require using two hands to pull the basket up and out.  If you're using the water filtration system, you will probably want to remove this outer basket for every pot, because you must put ground coffee (inside a paper or permanent filter) into the basket, and then place the water filtration system over the top of the basket.  The water filtration system itself consists of a charcoal filter disk that slides into a frame or lid for the "removable filter basket".  The water filtration system is supposed to remove chlorine from your water, to make your coffee taste better.  You need to change the charcoal filter every month.  (The coffeemaker comes with just one filter.)  This isn't very useful to me, because my tap water often tastes odd because of organic matter.  Removing chlorine isn't enough, so I filter my coffee water with a different type of filter before adding it to the coffeemaker's water reservoir.  My coffeemaker arrived without either paper filters or the "Mr. Coffee nylon permanent filter" that is supposed to be included (according to the instructions).  I guess that the permanent filter got removed in the warehouse, because the box wasn't completely sealed when it arrived.  Some kind of additional filter is needed inside the "removable filter basket", or coffee grounds will escape into the carafe during brewing.  The coffeemaker comes with a short 26" cord, which is more or less standard for kitchen appliances now, especially heating appliances.  The cord arrives coiled inside the coffeemaker's base, and needs to be pulled out when you first set up the coffeemaker.  Because of the many bad design features, I rate it this Mr. Coffee coffeemaker (Model BVMC-JPX37-AM) at 2 stars ("I don't like it" on the official Amazon scale).

T-fal P31052 Polished Aluminum Pressure Canner / Cooker with 2 Racks and 3-PSI Settings Cookware, 22-Quart, Silver by T-fal   Pressure canner does NOT have a flat bottom on the inside or outside, which leads to the reasons I cannot use it and can only give it one star.  I am leaving feedback based on my experience using other pressure canners vs. the T-fal.  Since this canner doesn't have a flat bottom the jars do not fit flat, they lean and aren't stable.  Even when the canner is full of jars, the jars rock.  The instruction book says the only compatible heat sources are gas and electric coil.  I have neither and Electric coil is becoming obsolete.  I have a Presto pressure canner and can use it on my glass top stove because it has a flat bottom, the jars do not rock and are stable.  Although the instructions on this T-fal say only electric coil and gas, if it would have a flat bottom, it could probably be used on a glass top stove.  It would be great to use on a gas top or electric coil type stove for cooking only.  I wouldn't use it as a canner either way due to the instability and leaning of the canning jars.

Update(June 2012):  hmm, not happy with it, the non stick became sticky.  The coating obviously didn't last long. The finish of the wok is truely high class, compared with a lot of the 'non'stick' varieties out there.  We have been looking for a decent non-stick wok for almost a year: hunting from william sonoma, china town, crate and barrel and web sites. why this amount of passion?  we love cooking with a wok, but our recent wok, a non-stick, and despite the seasoning - it just did not work. With a lot of claims on the non-stick material peeling off and the danger it poses to our health.  We finally buckled as the cleaning and the sticking on the non-stick wok was just taking the enjoyment out of cooking.  I think you have to bash this wok around before feeling happens. We think the non stick finish is a lot better than the All Clad brand, which we noticed had started to peel a few applications.  After a couple of heavy duty sessions with our wok, including a dinner party of 6, we love it.  the only grip is that we wish it could be 2 inches deeper, as per traditional wok.  hence you can toss and stir fry like in a traditional wok, but we are living with it as it as the food is not sticking/burning and the cleaning is a dream.  Note that there is a 25 years warranty on this product so scan a copy of this invoice, note down the address that is attached to the wok and save it in your email box!  If it serves me for 25 years, I think I certainly got my return on the cost!  The 12 inch is not that wide, and I would highly recommend for a family of two to a party up to 8.

I selected this cookie jar with the idea of keeping it on the counter with baked things in it. We didn't have a cookie car and have been using a plastic bowl with a silicone lid to keep things fresh, and this looked like a decent solution. Unfortunately it falls short - the top does not seal properly.  I like the overall design. The ceramic jar itself is a good size, sufficient to hold a pack of store-bought cookies comfortably, and the lid rests on top. The lid has a silicone gasket that is designed to create a seal when the lid is pressed down, locking in the air and preventing the stuff inside from getting stale. The jar itself seems to be well made and I did not find any burrs, pits, bubbles or uncovered spots in the glaze, either on the jar or the lid.  The problem is that the lid never seals. I tried multiple times to get the lid to create the airtight seal and it just won't do it. I even tried flipping the gasket around, thinking that it had been put on inside out or upside down, but this didn't help at all and the lid still would not seal. After trying to get the lid to seal I picked up the jar and inverted it, and the lid fell right off (I was careful to do this such that I could catch the lid so it would not break).  I wanted to like this jar but the fact that it will not seal is a big issue. I can use it for kitchen utensils without the lid, but for a cookie jar this one doesn't cut it.

This deep pocket heated mattress pad for a king-sized bed is like a heating pad. There are two controls that plug in on the top of the mattress cover and have settings for warm, low, medium, and high heat. The warm setting is the default. I could not feel much heat from any of the settings except for the high setting. The high setting provided a very low and very nice warmth. There are two controls; one for each side of the bed. There is a slight heat smell during first use.  I have a king sized bed that has a tall mattress with a topper. I require 16" deep pockets and the pockets on this are large enough to handle that even though there is nothing on the packaging that says that. This cover works (longevity unknown) but the CONS make this a pass for me.  PROS - It provides a nice light heat on the highest setting that apparently shuts off after 10 hours. I did not test the 10-hour shutoff as I'm rarely in bed for more than 6 hours.  - There are two controls for both sides of the bed so the other bed partner can select a different heat setting.  - The mattress pad is attractive and comfortable to lay on (except for the area with the plugs).  - It has deep pockets for a tall king-size mattress and fits up to my mattress pocket size of 16".  CONS - The electrical connection should have been located on the headboard side of the pad and only on the rim (meaning pocket side) of the pad. Currently, the connection is located at the foot of the mattress pad (based on the label). Of course, I can turn the label upside down and face the connection toward the top (which is what I did to access an electric socket); however, the plug on both sides is on the top and can be felt as a lump if laying down in that area.  - The pad I received arrived with black smudges as if someone walked on the mattress pad. When I order a new product, I expect to receive it in pristine condition.  - There was some cotton sticking out of the trim (see photo).  This is a minor detail and can just be cut off, but it is another sign of sloppiness.  - Although the elastic section (pockets) fit over my mattress, the elastic is not gripping enough. This means, it is easy for the mattress pad to slide off the mattress unless I sandwich the remaining cloth between the mattress and the box spring.  - There is a bit of a heat smell during first use; not much but is to be expected.  - This unit has to be unplugged daily as there is no shutoff except for the claim that it shuts off after 10 hours, which I did not test.  - I would not count on being able to wash this successfully. It says I can wash this in a machine for 5 min but I personally wouldn't do that. I would suggest spot cleaning if needed.  In summary, this mattress pad works to produce very low heat on the highest setting. It will fit a king-sized bed with 16" to perhaps 17" deep pockets but the elastic is too loose to keep it on the bed unless it is wedged between the mattress and box spring. The location of the plugs is a problem and the dirty topper I received is an utter disappointment. The current price of $33.99 ($39.99 minus 15%) is very reasonable for this. A mattress pad without any heat for a king-sized bed is usually priced much higher. My recommendation is to pass on this pad and buy flannel sheets for the winter that keep one warm and toasty under the blankets. If a heated mattress pad is desired, I would say to look elsewhere.

Let me start by saying that unlike the first reviewer, my unit was shipped in the manufacturer original packaging (not double boxed). I thought this was a terrible way to send this item and Amazon was lucky that the unit was packaged well by the manufacturer. Now to the device itself... Like I mentioned, this is a very heavy device and I needed help getting it set up out of the box (warning to people who are not that strong). Once set up, the unit was surprisingly quiet and I also was surprised how much water the system collected from my basement (guess I never thought of my basement as that humid). The 70 pint container filled in just about 2 days and, again, I would warn anyone without much strength to empty it more frequently. Bottom line, great device, works as designed and super quiet, just heavy and poorly packaged for shipping. I would not buy this as a gift for anyone (as there is no privacy in the shipping).

IM SO CONFUSED- trying for the first time… In order to make solid suction connection- I had to place nozzle so far into the toilet drain.. which was horrifying in itself, pushing through all the items clogging the toilet to begin with- BUT, after nozzle finally sealed opening- Blue portion of the plunger was 90% into the drain hole.. OK fine. However, once unclogged and I backed plunger out.. I was horrified!! The entire plunger was full of toilet debris and of the worst kind!! How in the world could anyone think this was a good idea…??!!  If you think of this logically it’s like plowing into and through what you don’t want to plow through.. Then, having 20-30oz of whatever is clogging the toilet now, all scooped up into the blue elongated portion of plunger. So, when removing plunger itself- all of that “yuck” is inside the elongated hose… And it comes out and dumps back into the toilet?!! OMG- I must have faith that something didn’t work as expected because, if this is accurate- it should never be used again. Not to mention I can’t even fathom what the interior of the blue nozzle is covered in and no, I did not want to wash it and find out!! What makes no sense… I’ve used a standard size plunger and it worked perfectly on those unpleasant situations.. And this is on a standard size toilet mind you. So, how anyone could possibly appreciate what has to take place in order to get suction with this plunger- seems a bit mine boggling.  I’m not going to experience this again and therefore, I am going to discard this and go back to the tried and true original style and be happy about it. To each his own… Just know, this is One of those things I’d like to not have to talk about again! Sorry so Crass- hoping this info can help you avoid a yucky experience!!

See first two photos - taken at highest heat setting of 8.  I have not encountered any issues with beeping or smoking. The lift feature just lifts the bread a fraction of an inch. I still use wooden toaster tongs to grab my bread. If the heating elements on the center panel actually came on, I would probably give this 5 stars ---- so far...  Updated Review 4/30/2015: I was concerned about the one sided toasting and called T-fal customer service. The customer service representative informed me that the heating coils on the inside panel should come on unless the toaster is set to bagel. The toaster I received (and probably some of the units received by other reviewers) is defective. I have changed my rating to one star.  In addition, it seems that I am not the first person to receive this particular toaster. The toaster was only cushioned by foam on one end and there was space in the box for cushioning on the other end. This is a 14-piece non-stick aluminum set that includes:  One 5 qt / 4.75 L Covered Dutch Oven One 2 qt / 1.9 L Covered Saucepan One 1 qt / 0.95L Covered Saucepan One 10.25 in / 26 cm Covered Fry Pan (has Thermo-Spot heat indicator) One 8 in / 20 cm Fry Pan (has Thermo-Spot heat indicator) One "One Egg Wonder" 4 Plastic Measuring Spoons  The package states these pots have a safe non-stick coating with NO PFOA, NO LEAD, and NO Cadmium. It also indicates that the set can be used on a gas, coil/solid electric, ceramic, or halogen stove.  It is made in China and comes with a limited lifetime warranty.  I tested my set on a gas stove.  What I like about this set is that it has a large covered fry pan. Most fry pans do not have covers.  I also like the brick red color, the handles on the pots, and the tops on the lids which are easy to grip. The pots also wash well. However, I dislike a lot about this cookware.  - The lids do not fit snugly on the large pot or fry pan because the pans are sort of misshaped (not fully circular) causing a gap.  The two saucepan covers fit snugly, however.  - The cookware does not hold heat well.  - The 1 qt saucepan easily tips just by touching the handle unless the contents inside are heavy.  - I saw no change in the Thermo- Spot heat indicator of the two fry pans even after heating them up for 5 minutes; so that feature does not work.  - The package says this is oven safe up to 350 degrees (177 C).  Personally that is useless to me because I cook at 350 or above. Since many oven temperatures are not exact, it would be easy to go over 350 and the handles would most likely be destroyed.  -  For those who use a dishwasher, it says dishwasher safe on the box but the instructions say hand washing is recommended for the non-stick pots.  - The egg I cooked stuck to the non-stick coating of the "one egg wonder" although it did clean up pretty well after soaking.  - The pots also do not stack well because they slip around a lot.  As a side note, since the pots have a non-stick coating, those with pet birds have to be careful using this around them or they could die from the toxicity. The fumes the heated non-stick coating gives off are toxic to birds.  Had I purchased this cookware I would have been extremely upset and sent it back for a refund. This set is just barely serviceable and I cannot recommend this cookware.  It is both low end and low quality in my opinion.  In fact, I am stunned that others find this cookware favorable to them.

I cannot believe I have to pay for this. This is not worth more than 50 cents.  This comes in three pieces. The yellow silicone cover on the handle, the frame itself with the egg well, and the silicone "bowl or rim."   After washing for first use, submerge this poacher in boiling water up to rim of poacher and pour egg from a prep bowl into the egg well once the water begins to swirl. Cook for 2-4 minutes, drain, and serve.  It seems easy (see first four photos for instructions) but it is not.  First of all, when the water was ready, I poured the egg into the poacher and after a few seconds white foam (from the egg white) coagulated in the water. Once the egg was cooked, I gently lifted this from the pot. However, the yellow silicone handle kept sliding off so I had to keep re-attaching it. I finally squeezed it tightly while lifting the handle so it woud not fall off again. After the "egg well" drained as I lifted it from the pot, I turned over the poacher to release the egg.  Good luck. The egg is stuck relatively solid to the egg well. Only the yolk and top part of the egg white slipped out. I had to use a spoon to extricate the rest and it left some concrete residue behind.  I wanted to do another egg but the previous event clogged all the vents in the poacher.  It took me a couple of hours of soaking the poacher in Dawn Platinium and scrubbing it for about 15 minutes to remove the "concrete" residue. Not only was this a tremendous amout of work but it was not easy to remove the silicone rim from the poacher for washing and re-assembling.  In summary, this is an absolutely awful poacher. It would be best to just crack the egg into boiling water with vinegar and then removing it with a slotted spoon. I am glad I used a non-stick pan or I would have been scrubbing that into the wee hours.  THE VERDICT - This gets an F minus. Use only if you want to spend hours extracting one poached egg and working on the cleanup.
You get ONE washcloth for this amount. Not worth the money.

Ok... I haven't ever "juiced" before and thought this looked like a good, reliable brand as well as a substantial machine.  It is apparently a very well made machine. The first juice I made was a whole pineapple, a golden delicous apple, a piece of fresh ginger, one lime and about 8 clementines. It says to peel all citrus and pineapples and I did.  It made a whole pitcher full (pitcher that came with the machine) It was the best juice I've ever tasted.  However, I have to say, I rated 4 stars instead of 5 because it is a REAL stinker to clean.  But then I will guess MOST juicers are.  I did purchase the pulp bucket liners and that helps, but it takes a significant amount of time and effort.  And I would also STRONGLY suggest that you wash it IMMEDIATELY after juicing to make it easier to clean. Allowing it to dry would make it nearly impossible to clean. I purchased some glass jars to freeze and refridgerate juices I have made.  Over all, I am enjoying the fresh juice when I am feeling energetic enough to do some serious cleaning.

If not, I will need to buy a new carpet and I would like to charge Bissell for it.  I also find it goes through a ton of water. As humidifiers go, I think this a good one.  For air quality safety they recommend daily cleaning.  I admit I do it about once a week... using vinegar and water.  This helps to kills fungus, mold and other bacteria.  I purchased two for my tiny house... one for the living area and one for the bedroom.  I ran them 24/7 for about a week and saw a very, very small improvement in the actual humidity of the air.  So... I have since stopped using them unless something has a head cold or something.  And I wash before every fill.  Allowing them to thoroughly dry before putting away.  They are a good size, taking a very small foot print area.  On the "low setting" I got over 10 hours of run time.  You can "see" the cool steam and the top has turnable direction flat so you can point the steam in the direction desired.  It makes very little noise while running.  All in all, if you want to run an occasional humidifier, this one will most likely work.  If you want to run one that will actually raise your humidity, you might want to consider something more commercial?  That is just my opinion. This is a great cup warmer as cup warmers go...  Christmas decor (green tree version) PROS -No on/off switch - comes on with the weight of the cup -keeps liquid warmish - but not hot (which may be a "con" for some folks). A warmer isn't really designed to keep liquids scalding hot - just warm -easy to clean smooth cup area. Clean by moist wipe - when cool, not hot -festive holiday print - I'll use year round CONS -pretty big foot print - if you have a smaller area, this is gonna be a challenge... about 5 1/2" across the middle of circle There is a black cord to plug in - about 3ft or so long.  The warmer has a lit ring that is lit when there is weight on the warmer - activating the the warmer which gets pretty hot - but does keep liquids warmish - not hot.  The other thing to note is that you will need a flat bottom cup.  It will not warm any cup that isn't fully flat on the warmer. - The bottom of the warmer is plastic and will be slippery on other plastic surfaces.  I tried to put some of that rubber webbing underneath, but the warmer won't turn on because the weight of the cup of presses a tiny button on the bottom of the unit doesn't reach the surface you set it on - and therefore wont turn on.  This is the biggest challenge so far. Over all, in my opinion, it works as I'd expected. I hate to drink tepid coffee or tea - this does make it warmer than tepid.  The less liquid that it is in the cup, the warmer it will be (captain obvious - right?). To me, this is a nice quality warmer for a reasonable price.  Having said all of that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Ok... Here is what I think about this ... In the end, it's not for me... but I try to be fair in my rating. PROS: -It baked scrambled egg mixture  (I added a bit of cheese, green onions and about 1 tbls of 1/2 and 1/2) - NOTE:  2 eggs is two cooking cycles as it fills 7 cups - you can cook 4 mini cups or 1 big cup at a time.  It did take about 11 minutes (10-12 is on the instructions) for each batch.  Also - you should know the base will turn off and on several times during cooking - at first I thought it had died. -I spritzed a tiny bit of olive oil in each mini cup and they popped right out - Clean up was an easy wipe with a warm paper towel AFTER it had cooled off. - I haven't tried the custard recipe.. but maybe this would be the best of this unit - because it would make little tiny custard or cheesecake bites - again tho, for 1 or 2. CONS... - Unless you're cooking for just one, it will take a very, very long time to get breakfast done. - Cooking was a bit irregular in temp... the bite (mini cups) are about 2" in diameter and about 1/2" thick.  A couple were not fully cooked inside - while others were very cooked. In my personal opinion... it takes me about 3-5 minutes to scramble some eggs for myself and another person.  Sure, it's not fancy little bites... but I am in and out of the kitchen even with cleanup!  So... while this is a cute little unit.. it's bit too much work (mostly in cooking time) for the payoff.  Just my opinion.  Why 5 stars?  Because it works and I try very hard not to rate on personal opinion.

This would have been a great five-compartment organizer to store different types of Ziplock bags (quart, gallon, sandwich, and snack) and a roll of either foil, plastic wrap, or wax paper. Unfortunately, it has three design flaws.  FLAWS  - First, mine arrived with a crack in the base (see photo).  - Second, the entire unit opens, which means everything falls out when reloading it while on the wall. Bags can be loaded through the holes easily, however.  - Third, because the unit opens for reloading, the roll has to constantly be re-threaded through the dispenser slot on the bottom. There are other units that keep that compartment separate (either via a hole on the side or a separate door) for that reason. Because there is no way to replace the roll without opening this unit, it is subject to the 2nd design flaw above.  This comes with two mounting screws with plastic mollies needed to hang this on a wall. If putting this in a drawer, it has rubberized feet affixed to the bottom to prevent scratches or prevent it from sliding around.  Overall, this is still a functional organizer that does away with all those squished and ugly boxes in my cabinets and perhaps one roll of foil. However, it is less functional when hung and the longevity due to the cracked back and security of the hinges is questionable. The current price of $22.99 is commensurate with the low quality of this unit.  There are many other Ziplock bag organizer options available on the market such as this [[ASIN:B09KMNHM2J one]] or this [[ASIN:B09VGP1WRL one]]. For one full unit only, then I recommend this [[ASIN:B09RF759BM rustic organizer]]. For a more modern look, I recommend this [[ASIN:B09WN5PZ6Q one]] or an individual heavy plastic set like [[ASIN:B09THNTBGX this]]. For a larger unit that has room for foil and plastic wrap, then I highly recommend this [[ASIN:B0B1TPD21S one]]. I tried all of these and the style purchased is based on what one desires. I personally like these other ones a little better than this one.

As I have gotten older, it's less comfortable.  Now, I have just begun using these and I hope that they will retain a charge for a reasonable time.  Also, I did have to put a plug extender on my kitchen counter that also included USB ports.  I didn't want to have to take them to my bedroom to charge when they needed it.  They are fairly heavy - partly because of the glass bottoms.  They recommend not filling more than 80% to make sure they grind properly.  I followed that advice.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. UPDATE:  2/20/2021... Just a quick note.  I have found that when you turn over the grinders, they start.  Initially they did not. I had high expectations for these shears but was sorely disappointed and here is why:  - The shears don't cut well at all unless I put the herb stem all the way to the base of the shear (that's the intersection of the cutting blades) .  The tip has minimal cutting power unless the item being cut is delicate -- like cutting chives.  - The handle lock requires force to close.  - The herb stripper slot is awkward to use, especially for items with bigger leaves that have to be "fed through the handle" in order to reach the slot. Although I was partially successful in removing thyme from its branch that only worked when the branch wasn't delicate enough to break on its own.  The only thing I like about these shears is that the rubberized handle feels good in my hand and provides me with a non-slip grip when the shears are open.  I cannot recommend these and would purchase kitchen shears instead.  This is another item that will eventually head to the landfill. I will continue to use my thumb and forefinger to strip herbs as I always do and will use my standard kitchen shears to do all the cutting work.

I had gotten the Ninja blender and it worked great for a while. I really liked - past tense - the locking lid, but cleaning it by throwing it in the dishwasher or washing it by hand was futile.  I had been using my blender for many months when I started noticing a rotten smell that was nauseating. After a little thought, I figured out the milk that I was using to make smoothies and milkshakes was collecting within the inside of the lid. I almost threw it out and got a new lid. Instead, I decided to try and fix it, which ended up being a real pain in the neck. I had to take the lid apart, piece by piece, and wash every piece in there that were covered in dry old milk residue. I had to do this for DAYS before I was able to *mostly* get the rotten smell out. (Note that I said MOSTLY). I put it back together and attached the fixed lid onto the pitcher. I was able to lock it without a problem, but I haven't used it since I did that. It's functional, but I don't want to have to go through that again, since this will inevitably happen again.

[[VIDEOID:fc1e9bcb5d0203dbb23da4974b4c9270]] I both love and hate this fireplace. As you can see from the video and picture, it is absolutely gorgeous in white. It is also small. I also was only interested in the ambiance of the real-looking log fire made by the lights. However, the entire unit should work whether I wanted it to heat a room or not.  Set-up was easy. Reading the manual actually makes one wonder whether it should be plugged in or not because there are all sorts of overheating and fire warnings that made me nervous. Nevertheless, I tried it. The fireplace was gorgeous and realistic when lit. However, the 1500W heater would not go on. The 750W heater worked. As a result, I had my boyfriend return this as defective and decided against a replacement.  I am so disappointed but after reading the manual, some of the other reviews, and having experienced a defective unit on my first try; it is best I not get a replacement. The rack was not padded in the box in any way, but managed to arrive undamaged, except for the warehouse forgetting to include the screws.  If you don't have to deal with the seller for some reason, you will be very pleased with the rack I believe.  It is very sturdy.  Folds up so nicely and conveniently. A tag inside suggested keeping about 1/3 of a cup of water in the resting cup to keep the honey from crystalizing at the tip of the reservoir.  An interesting suggestion... EDIT:  Rating downgraded from 5 stars to 2 stars.  Sad to say, this can opener stopped working properly after about six months of light use.  The jaws started opening out of sequence, ran even more slowly than before, and would no longer open most jars.  Changing the batteries didn't help.  The opener had not been dropped or obviously damaged in any way.  My husband went back to using his manual [[ASIN:B000079XW2 OXO Good Grips Smooth Edge Can Opener]], which is relatively easy to operate one-handed, and is highly recommended.  ORIGINAL REVIEW: This amazing Hamilton Beach Automatic Jar Opener easily opens jars with 1" to 4" diameter lids.  I call it amazing, because you wouldn't expect a device powered by only two AA batteries to have the "grip strength" to open tightly sealed pickle jars!  I tested this opener on both large and small pickle jars that always make me use a tool to break the seal, a peanut butter jar, and an extremely hard-to-open prune juice bottle.  It worked every time.  All you need to do, is hold the jar steady, place and hold the opener on top of the jar lid, and press the "Open Jar" button.  This activates the opener's two sets of gripper arms (which move quite slowly).  The first set moves to grasp the jar; then the second set moves in to grip and rotate the lid.  When the jar is open, you press the "Release Lid" button.  The opener then releases the lid and automatically returns its gripper arms to the proper position for storage and/or the next use.  The instructions say that this jar opener is intended to open only glass jars with metal lids, and NOT intended to open plastic jars.  However, it opened my plastic peanut butter jar and plastic prune juice bottle without difficulty.  Just keep in mind that you use the opener on plastic jars at your own risk.  Perhaps some plastic jars are too fragile to withstand the force applied by the gripper arms and jaws?  My husband, who has full use of only one hand, is able to open jars easily with this gadget.  He holds the jars in a vise in a kitchen workstation designed for one-handed cooks (e.g., [[ASIN:B000HZU06C Swedish Cutting Board]]), and operates the opener with his good hand.  Because this device is a great help to anyone who even occasionally has difficulty opening jars, I rate it at an unqualified 5 stars.

This INTERDESIGN 04953 WREN BEDSIDE STORAGE ORGANIZER (for Phone, Tablet, Magazines, Water Bottle, Remote Control - 4 Pockets, Light Gray) is just plain ugly.  It's made of heavy, coarse, taupe canvas that is stitched (and probably glued) to a stiff, rubberized ivory backing.  The fabric feels unpleasant to the touch.  The organizer doesn't hold thick items, because the large and medium pockets are stitched down almost flat to the backing.  Only the water bottle pocket has gussets to allow for insertion of a bulky item.  The product photo showing the pockets curving outward is misleading.  That mostly happens when items are inserted into the pockets, and then the organizer doesn't lie flat against the side of the bed.  The organizer installs easily by pushing the wire frame between the mattress and box springs, but it hangs almost to the floor on my bed.  (It needs 11" vertical clearance from the bottom of the mattress to the floor.)  As a result, the organizer is in the way of my feet when I get into bed, if I install it where it's easiest to reach when I'm in bed.  The between-the-mattress-and-box-springs installation means that the organizer (with its contents) must be removed whenever you change the sheets.  The organizer also gets in the way of your bedcovers, unless you install it at the very head of the bed.  The organizer can be easily removed from the wire frame for cleaning, because it is attached with a strip of heavy Velcro.  I was hoping to use this organizer to hold things that wouldn't fit on my crowded bedside table, but it's too inconvenient for regular use.  InterDesign products are usually much nicer than this one.

This HIMALAYAN SALT COMPANY PILLAR LAMP is mesmerizingly beautiful when turned on.  However, it is totally different from what I expected.  The lamp iS NOT a solid pillar of salt inside an ironwork base; instead it's a collection of loose lumps of salt inside an ironwork basket that is shaped like a pillar.  When the lamp arrived, the salt lumps were contained in a plastic bag inside the basket.  The bag had to be removed before the lamp could be used; the same was true of cardboard central core that protected the little 15-watt bulb (at the bottom of the basket) from breakage during shipping.  The only way to remove the bag was to carefully dump out the salt chunks.  Fortunately they were easy to replace, though they left a pile of loose salt behind.  The salt chunks were okay with me, but then I discovered that the lamp would NOT stand up straight.  The reason was that the connection between the light bulb and the electrical cord was contacting the countertop beneath the lamp.  That connection was longer than the three little ball-shaped legs--so the electrical cord connection was taking the place of one leg, and tilting the whole lamp to one side.  I’m NOT comfortable, sleeping or working next to an electric lamp with such a poorly designed base.  This is all the more true because the lamp is supposed to be turned on for long hours to get the promised therapeutic effect.  If I’d looked closely at the product page photo before ordering, I’d have realized the salt came in chunks; and I have to admit that the chunks probably add to the lamp’s beauty when it's lighted up.  However, there is no easy fix possible for the “fourth leg” on the black basket-shaped base, and I don't trust the safety of this electric lamp.

My husband loves home-made pies, so I thought it would be fun to make mini-pies from time to time, just to change things up a little.  After reading the Amazon reviews for various competing mini-pie makers, I ordered this well-reviewed SUNBEAM 4-PIECE PIE MAKER.  Little did I know what it had in store for me!  I followed the booklet's recipe for blueberry mini-pies to the letter.  I lightly oiled the pie cups, preheated the pie maker, used the provided tool to cut top and bottom crusts from Pillsbury ready-made crusts, prepared the filling, used the provided tool to press the bottom crusts into the pie maker, measured 1/2 cup of filling into each bottom crust, added the top crusts, and clamped the top of the pie maker shut.  The pie maker began emitting steam, which seemed odd.  After 10 minutes, the pie crusts were done.  However, the pie filling had boiled out onto the pie-maker plates, and a lot of it had spilled down the outside and into the hinges.  Because the pie-maker plates were hot, the filling was busily burning onto the plates.  As I was removing the mini-pies, the bottom of one burst and dropped all its filling.  The final straw was my husband's comment that his intact mini-pie seemed "kind of filled with air"!  Oh well.  Maybe I should have used one of the booklet's two recipes for pie crusts--although the booklet did say you could use ready-made crusts from the grocery store.  Or maybe I should have crimped the top and bottom crusts together--although the instructions did not say to do this, and the plates look like they will do the crimping when you close the lid.  The cleanup was such a nightmare, I have no desire to ever use this appliance again.  The mini-pies are cute, but the preparation time is about the same as for a full-sized, double-crust blueberry pie (except that a 9" pie needs 45 minutes to bake).  Also, the mini-pies don't hold much filling, especially if you have to use less than 1/2 cup of filling.  I rate this Sunbeam 4-piece pie maker at 2 stars ("I don't like it" on the official Amazon scale).

I really love good white noise and sleep with a fan sound every night. And I’ve seen reviews of purifiers in the past that claimed they were too loud, and I thought that wouldn’t ever be an issue for me personally. But this purifier annoys me. The sound isn’t a good white noise. It’s more of a hum. And even level 2 is way too loud to be running while I’m working from home in the same room. The small light on this is obnoxiously bright too, so at night, even after putting it on the night setting, I have to put a pack of gum over the light to cover it up. I can easily sleep with a normal lamp or bathroom light on, but this light is unnecessarily harsh. I’m not even sure why it needs a light at all really. So even though I love how the filter is only supposed to be changed yearly (as opposed to the every 4 month replacements on the Filtrete Large Room with True Hepa purifier I have), I find it too annoying to use at night or past level 1 during the day. I do enjoy the Filtrete one from Amazon though and it has great reviews at a reasonable price. And it’s not loud or overly bright. I’d recommend checking it out!

I've had other 1 cup brewers and a Grinder & Brew types and you could easily make 2-3 cups in a row. Not with this one. After EACH cup, every removeable part requires cleaning! The grinder builds up a lot of grinds in the screen and must be cleaned and flushed. The lid for the grinder gets build up in nooks and crannies that are almost impossible to clean. The reusable filter needs cleaning and flushing. By the time you are ready to make another cup, the first cup is tepid! I don't know how it was possible for a company like Cuisinart to make such a ridiculous machine! My other grind and brew ground the beans and brewed the coffee and to make another cup, all that was required was to empty the reuseable filter and rinse and make another cup. It is such a hassle with the Cuisinart that it sits on the counter unused and waiting until I can get one of my old brands back. I needed to revisit my review... Upon using this machine again two times, I found that even with brushes, it is nearly impossible to get the weird angles in the grinding and brewing components clean. Soaking won't do it and sponges can't clean it. Takes 5 minutes to make a cup of coffee, ten minutes to clean it properly for another cup.

This item is the perfect size for hanging in a bedroom! It’s not too small but not so big that it overwhelms the room. It is a bit smaller than the pictures make it look so pay attention to the measurements of the unit and also before buying knickknacks etc. for the individual shelves. The shelf as a whole seems to be well made. My only wish is that is has pre-drilled slots in the wood To hang up with instead of the clip style used but other wise worth it!

Easy to set, easy to use and the alarm that starts low and gets louder is PERFECT for sleepers like me.  It's like the old days, again as it's easy to set and use with none of the frills most people want today.  Price was good as well.  The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is because it doesn't work on an AM or PM mode, if you want to get up at 8:00 whether AM or in the PM, you must as an example hit the alarm button at 9:00 or else the alarm with ring anytime within that hour - you'll see what I mean if you buy this.  I still love it as it's perfect to get me up and moving.
